mission.title: Install and Configure Dalec on Kubernetes
mission.description: Dalec is a project aimed at providing a declarative format for building system packages and containers with a focus on supply chain security. Installing Dalec allows you to produce secure packages and containers efficiently using simple configurations.

steps.title: Add the Dalec Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the Dalec Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration with the following command:
```bash
helm repo add dalec https://project-dalec.github.io/dalec/charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Dalec using Helm
steps.description:
Install Dalec in the 'dalec' namespace (it will be created if it doesn't exist) with the following command:
```bash
helm install dalec dalec/dalec --namespace dalec --create-namespace --version v0.20.3
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Dalec pods are running successfully:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace dalec
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
To ensure optimal performance, configure resource limits for the Dalec deployment. Edit the deployment with:
```bash
kubectl edit deployment dalec --namespace dalec
``` 
Then add the following under the containers section:
```yaml
resources:
  limits:
    cpu: "500m"
    memory: "512Mi"
  requests:
    cpu: "250m"
    memory: "256Mi"
```

steps.title: Set up port forwarding for access
steps.description:
To access the Dalec service, set up port forwarding:
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/dalec --namespace dalec 8080:80
``` 
You can now access Dalec at http://localhost:8080.

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Dalec will show the pods running when you execute `kubectl get pods --namespace dalec`. You can also access the Dalec service through port forwarding, confirming that the installation was successful.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add dalec https://project-dalec.github.io/dalec/charts
helm repo update
helm install dalec dalec/dalec --namespace dalec --create-namespace --version v0.20.3

resources:
  limits:
    cpu: "500m"
    memory: "512Mi"
  requests:
    cpu: "250m"
    memory: "256Mi"

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Dalec release from the 'dalec' namespace using the following command:
```bash
helm uninstall dalec --namespace dalec
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and persistent resources
uninstall.description:
Remove any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) and persistent volume claims associated with Dalec:
```bash
kubectl delete crd $(kubectl get crd | grep dalec | awk '{print $1}')
kubectl delete pvc --all --namespace dalec
```

uninstall.title: Remove namespace and verify
uninstall.description:
Delete the 'dalec' namespace and verify it has been removed:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace dalec
kubectl get namespaces | grep dalec
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up the current configuration and state of Dalec:
```bash
helm get values dalec --namespace dalec > dalec-backup-values.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update repo and run upgrade command
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repository and upgrade Dalec to the latest version:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade dalec dalec/dalec --namespace dalec --version v0.21.0
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check that the Dalec pods are running the new version successfully:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace dalec
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If the Dalec pods are in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for errors:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace dalec
``` 
Look for any error messages that indicate what might be wrong.

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot access the Dalec service, ensure the port forwarding is set up correctly:
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/dalec --namespace dalec 8080:80
``` 
Also, check the service status:
```bash
kubectl get svc --namespace dalec
```

troubleshooting.title: Insufficient resources
troubleshooting.description:
If the pods are not starting due to resource issues, check the resource requests and limits:
```bash
kubectl describe deployment dalec --namespace dalec
``` 
Adjust the resource limits if necessary.

troubleshooting.title: Helm release not found
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter an error stating that the Helm release is not found, verify the release name and namespace:
```bash
helm list --namespace dalec
``` 
Ensure you are using the correct release name 'dalec'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have Kubernetes cluster access and have installed Helm and kubectl.
